FLEX.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
FLEX.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

HUT.headline: Hut 8, Cipher Digital Stocks Boom As AI Data Center Deals Fuel Rally
HUT.description: Hut 8 and Cipher Digital stocks surged on strong AI data center lease announcements. Hut 8 signed a 15-year, $9.8 billion deal (potentially $25.1 billion with renewals) for 352 MW of AI capacity, while Cipher Digital signed its third AI data center campus lease with a hyperscale tenant. Both companies are pivoting from crypto mining to high-performance computing infrastructure, attracting investor interest in contracted capacity and long-duration AI revenue.

SMCI.headline: Super Micro Computer Ignites AI Rally With Strong Sales and Earnings Guidance
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) surged 22.44% after reporting quarterly sales of $10.24 billion (122.6% YoY growth) and providing forward guidance of $11.75 billion in sales and 72 cents EPS, both exceeding analyst expectations. The company's operating margins expanded to 6.1% from 3.2% YoY. Memory-related stocks like Micron Technology and Seagate Technology also rallied amid the AI surge. The positive ADP payroll report (109,000 jobs created in April) supports expectations for strong employment dat

SMCI.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
SMCI.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

SMCI.headline: Why Super Micro Computer Stock Soared Today
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer stock surged 16.6% after beating earnings expectations with $0.84 per share versus forecasted $0.62, despite missing on sales ($10.2B vs. $12.4B expected). However, the company's free cash flow has dramatically deteriorated to negative $7.7 billion year-to-date from positive $690 million last year, raising concerns about sustainability despite strong AI server demand and projected $38.9-40.4 billion in annual sales.

SMCI.headline: AI Earnings Reaction: AMD Leads, SMCI Survives, Nasdaq Now Must Prove It
SMCI.description: AMD delivered strong AI earnings with a 16%+ overnight rally, driven by data center growth and EPYC/Instinct products. Super Micro Computer (SMCI) rallied 15% on forward guidance despite missing revenue. Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) remained range-bound. The Nasdaq-100 faces a critical test: whether the gap-up from positive AI earnings can hold or if the move represents exhaustion and profit-taking.

SMCI.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Rise As Trump Pauses 'Project Freedom'— Super Micro Computer, AMD, Intel In Focus  (UPDATED)
SMCI.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following President Trump's announcement of a temporary pause in 'Project Freedom,' a military operation escorting commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones advanced 0.59% and 0.66% respectively. Tech stocks led gains, with Super Micro Computer jumping 17.21% on strong earnings, AMD surging 18.64% on better-than-expected results, and Intel advancing 6.40% after clearing an antitrust hurdle. The Fed is expected to hold rates stea

SMCI.headline: ChronoScale Appoints Cenly Chen as Chief Executive Officer to Lead Next Phase of AI Compute Growth
SMCI.description: ChronoScale Corporation announced the appointment of Cenly Chen as Chief Executive Officer, bringing over two decades of enterprise infrastructure and high-performance computing experience from Super Micro Computer. The appointment comes as demand for AI compute infrastructure accelerates, with ChronoScale positioning itself to expand capacity and deliver consistent performance at scale.

SMCI.headline: Why Is Super Micro Stock Soaring Wednesday?
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) reported mixed Q3 results with EPS beating expectations at 84 cents vs. 62 cents consensus, but revenue missing at $10.24B vs. $12.33B expected. The company is experiencing strong AI infrastructure growth with over 80% of revenue from AI GPU platforms. SMCI issued upbeat Q4 guidance with EPS of 65-79 cents and revenue of $11-12.5B, driving shares up 17.32% in premarket trading.

SMCI.headline: Super Micro Computer, Inc. Notice of May 26, 2026 Application Deadline for Class Action Lawsuits - Contact Lewis Kahn, Esq. at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, Before Application Deadline
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer faces class action lawsuits following a Department of Justice indictment against three company-associated individuals for allegedly diverting $2.5 billion worth of AI servers to China in violation of U.S. export control laws. The disclosure triggered a 33.3% stock price decline on March 20, 2026. Investors who suffered losses between February 2, 2024 and March 19, 2026 have until May 26, 2026 to request lead plaintiff status.

SMCI.headline: Super Micro Stock Spikes After Q3 Earnings Beat, Strong Q4 Guidance
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) reported Q3 earnings of 84 cents per share, beating consensus estimates by 35.48%, though revenue of $10.24 billion missed expectations. The company issued strong Q4 guidance with adjusted EPS of 65-79 cents and revenue of $11-12.5 billion, both exceeding analyst estimates. The stock surged 16.56% in after-hours trading.

SMCI.headline: SMCI Investor Alert: Super Micro Computer Securities Fraud Lawsuit - Investors With Losses May Seek to Lead the Class Action After Allegedly Misrepresenting Revenue Derived from Sales Potentially Violating U.S. Export Controls: Levi & Korsinsky
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) faces a securities class action lawsuit after the DOJ indicted its co-founder for allegedly diverting approximately $2.5 billion in restricted AI servers to China without proper export licenses. The company had projected $33-36 billion in FY2026 revenue while allegedly concealing material compliance violations. SMCI shares plunged 33.3% ($10.26 per share) on March 20, 2026, following the indictment disclosure. Investors who purchased securities between April 30, 2024

DVA.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
DVA.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

JOBY.headline: Why Did Joby Aviation Stock Fly Today?
JOBY.description: Joby Aviation stock surged 17.2% after reporting Q1 2026 results, highlighting operational achievements including successful eVTOL flight demonstrations and expected commercial operations later in 2026. However, the company remains pre-revenue and analysts project cash burn could deplete its $2.5 billion reserves by 2029, two years before achieving profitability.

AMD.headline: Michael Burry's Chip Short Just Got Wilder: SOXX Went From 43% Above Trend To 60%
AMD.description: Michael Burry's short position on the semiconductor sector ETF SOXX has become increasingly challenged as the fund rallied to $502, now trading 60% above its 200-day moving average—the highest gap since the ETF's inception in 2001. Burry purchased January 2027 puts at $330 strike when SOXX was at $455, requiring a 35% drawdown to break even. The extreme valuation stretch, combined with nine constituents trading even further above their trend lines, suggests either a sideways consolidation or sha

AMD.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 Surge: AMD Earnings Steal the Show at Midday
AMD.description: Stock markets rallied on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 up 1.3% and Nasdaq-100 up 1.7%, driven by AMD's blowout Q1 earnings report showing strong AI accelerator demand. AMD stock surged 17.5%, boosting the broader semiconductor sector including Nvidia (+5.3%). Oil prices dropped over 6% on reports of U.S.-Iran peace negotiations, though President Trump tempered deal expectations.

AMD.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
AMD.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

AMD.headline: AMD’s Upside Movement Has Just Begun After Years in the Making
AMD.description: AMD is positioned to accelerate revenue growth as it becomes a key player in AI infrastructure alongside NVIDIA. The company's Q1 earnings exceeded expectations with 37.8% revenue growth, strong margins, and positive guidance. With the MI450 GPU launch and Helios rack-scale systems expected in Q3, major hyperscalers like Meta, Google, and Microsoft are committing to AMD products. Analysts see significant upside potential, with AMD's valuation suggesting 500-600% growth potential over time as its

AMD.headline: AMD Just Blew Out Earnings -- and Wall Street Can't Get Enough. Analyst Price Targets Are Skyrocketing
AMD.description: AMD reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with revenue of $10.25 billion (beating estimates) and 38% year-over-year growth, driven by its data center division which grew 57% YoY. The company provided robust Q2 guidance of $11.2 billion in revenue. Multiple Wall Street analysts significantly raised their price targets by $200-$300, citing strong demand for AI infrastructure and agentic AI workloads. The stock surged 16.84% on the news.

AMD.headline: AI Earnings Reaction: AMD Leads, SMCI Survives, Nasdaq Now Must Prove It
AMD.description: AMD delivered strong AI earnings with a 16%+ overnight rally, driven by data center growth and EPYC/Instinct products. Super Micro Computer (SMCI) rallied 15% on forward guidance despite missing revenue. Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) remained range-bound. The Nasdaq-100 faces a critical test: whether the gap-up from positive AI earnings can hold or if the move represents exhaustion and profit-taking.

AMD.headline: Welcome to Shareholder Meeting Month: AI, Oil, and Consumer Are in the Spotlight
AMD.description: May marks Shareholder Meeting Month with major corporations holding Annual General Meetings across sectors including Consumer Staples, Industrials, Financials, Tech, and Energy. Key topics include consumer spending resilience, AI strategy, energy production amid geopolitical tensions, and economic health. Notable meetings include PepsiCo, Southwest Airlines, AMD, Intel, JPMorgan, Citigroup, Amazon, McDonald's, Exxon Mobil, Chevron, and Salesforce, offering investors forward-looking insights beyo

AMD.headline: AMD Just Soared 20% on Earnings. Is It Still a Buy or Time to Take Profits?
AMD.description: AMD surged approximately 20% following strong Q1 2026 earnings results, with the company stepping out of Nvidia's shadow. The article discusses whether the stock remains a buy or if investors should take profits, while also covering earnings reports from Oscar Health and Uber.

AMD.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Rise As Trump Pauses 'Project Freedom'— Super Micro Computer, AMD, Intel In Focus  (UPDATED)
AMD.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following President Trump's announcement of a temporary pause in 'Project Freedom,' a military operation escorting commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones advanced 0.59% and 0.66% respectively. Tech stocks led gains, with Super Micro Computer jumping 17.21% on strong earnings, AMD surging 18.64% on better-than-expected results, and Intel advancing 6.40% after clearing an antitrust hurdle. The Fed is expected to hold rates stea

AMD.headline: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down On Wednesday?
AMD.description: The S&P 500 climbed to a record high on Tuesday, rising 0.81% to 7,259.22, driven by easing oil prices, strong corporate earnings (84% beat expectations), and positive geopolitical sentiment. Polymarket shows 88% odds of an up open on Wednesday. S&P 500 futures were higher early Wednesday, with semiconductor stocks and AI-linked trades providing momentum.

IFF.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
IFF.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. IFF.headline: IEP Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
IFF.description: Icahn Enterprises reported a Q1 2026 net loss of $459 million ($0.71 per unit) with adjusted EBITDA loss of $216 million, primarily due to $425 million in refining hedge losses and $158 million in unrealized derivative losses. Leadership transitioned from Andrew Teno to Ted Papapostolou as CEO. The investment funds returned 4.4% excluding hedges but -8.2% including them. Portfolio positions showed mixed results with several holdings posting gains, while operating segments faced headwinds from re

RIOT.headline: Stock Market Today, May 5: Cipher Digital Surges on AI Data Center Shift Backed by Hyperscale Leases
RIOT.description: Cipher Mining (CIFR) surged 23.53% on May 5, 2026, despite weak Q1 results, driven by a strategic pivot toward AI and high-performance computing data centers. The company secured a new $200 million credit facility and announced approximately $11.4 billion in contracted revenue, signaling a shift from bitcoin mining to data center infrastructure.

OKLO.headline: 3 Next Generation Nuclear Energy Stocks Investors Should Be Watching Closely
OKLO.description: As global energy demand surges, three nuclear energy companies are positioned to lead the next-generation reactor market. Oklo develops advanced microreactors with strong customer demand but faces multi-year delays before operations. NuScale holds regulatory approval as the only certified SMR developer but needs to convert partnerships into binding deals. GE Vernova, an established power company, is actively constructing its first commercial SMR in Canada with near-term revenue from gas turbines

IREN.headline: IREN Announces Acquisition of Mirantis to Strengthen AI Cloud Delivery Capabilities
IREN.description: IREN Limited (NASDAQ:IREN) announced a definitive agreement to acquire Mirantis, Inc., a cloud infrastructure and Kubernetes orchestration provider, for approximately $625 million in IREN ordinary shares. The acquisition aims to enhance IREN's AI Cloud services by strengthening deployment, management, and operational capabilities. Mirantis brings enterprise support expertise and serves over 1,500 global customers, while its k0rdent AI platform will help manage AI infrastructure across various en

AUR.headline: How a Berkshire Hathaway Company Helped Aurora Innovation Stock Soar Today
AUR.description: Aurora Innovation announced a partnership with Berkshire Hathaway's McClane Company to expand autonomous delivery services in Texas, building on a successful pilot program that delivered 1,400 loads with 100% on-time delivery. The company also expanded its self-driving program with Volvo Group, launching a new 200-mile route between Dallas and Oklahoma City. Aurora's stock surged 8.73% on the news.

STRL.headline: Sterling Infrastructure Stock Hits 52-Week High - Here's Why
STRL.description: Sterling Infrastructure (NASDAQ: STRL) reported strong Q1 2026 results with revenue of $825.7 million (up 92% YoY) and adjusted EPS of $3.59, significantly exceeding analyst expectations. The company raised its full-year 2026 guidance to $18.40-$19.05 EPS and $3.7-$3.8 billion revenue. The CEC acquisition contributed substantially to growth, and backlog reached $5.15 billion. Shares surged 29.46% in premarket trading to a new 52-week high.

CRCL.headline: Is XRP Too Risky to Own -- or Too Cheap to Ignore?
CRCL.description: XRP trades near $1.40 despite analyst forecasts suggesting potential for 10x returns by 2027, driven by Ripple's blockchain payment infrastructure and regulatory clarity. However, the emergence of stablecoins like Ripple USD is siphoning value away from XRP, and most value from Ripple's infrastructure may flow to the company itself rather than the token. The author recommends avoiding XRP in favor of publicly traded fintech companies like Coinbase and Circle Internet Group that are building bloc

POET.headline: Should You Buy the Dip in Poet Technologies Stock?
POET.description: Poet Technologies, a photonic solutions specialist for AI data centers, has seen its stock plummet 70% from all-time highs following a confidentiality dispute over a customer order from Marvell Technology's subsidiary. While the company's optical interconnect technology shows promise for addressing AI infrastructure challenges, Poet remains unprofitable with minimal revenue and significant execution risks, making it a highly speculative investment suitable only for risk-tolerant investors.

POET.headline: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges POET Technologies Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
POET.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against POET Technologies Inc. alleging securities fraud. The complaint claims the company misrepresented its tax status as a potential passive foreign investment company (PFIC), failed to disclose negative tax implications for U.S. shareholders, and that an officer violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased POET securities between April 1-27, 2026.

POET.headline: POET Technologies Stock Climbs As Oil Slides And Ceasefire Holds
POET.description: POET Technologies shares surged 31.08% on Tuesday as semiconductor stocks rallied broadly. The gains were driven by easing geopolitical tensions following reassurances about the Iran ceasefire, which caused oil prices to drop significantly. Additionally, semiconductor stocks benefited from reports of U.S. efforts to address global memory chip shortages through the Pax Silica initiative, a supply-chain coalition with allied nations.

POET.headline: Is Poet Technologies a Millionaire-Maker Stock?
POET.description: Poet Technologies, a small-cap AI infrastructure company focused on photonic semiconductor technology, shows potential for disruptive innovation but faces significant risks. The company lost a major $5 million contract from Marvell Technology's Celestial AI subsidiary in late April, raising concerns about commercial viability. While 2025 revenue surged 2,500% to $1.07 million, this growth stems from a tiny base and the company faces mounting operating losses ($42.1 million in 2025). Analysts rec
